description: ASCE 7-16 Wind Load Chatbot — Conversation Flow Schema

phases.name: Building Classification
phases.goal: Derive Risk Category from occupancy questions — never ask user for Risk Category directly

questions.text: What is the primary use of this building? (e.g. office, warehouse, retail, school, hospital, residential)
questions.why: Used to map occupancy type and derive Risk Category per ASCE 7-16 Table 1.5-1
questions.llm_action: Map user response to occupancy_type_map in risk_category.json — extract best matching key

questions.text: Approximately how many people occupy the building at peak times?
questions.why: Occupant load affects Risk Category — buildings with 300+ occupants or assembly use are Risk Cat III

questions.text: Is this building designated as essential for post-disaster response? (e.g. hospital, fire station, emergency operations center, water treatment facility)
questions.why: Essential facilities are Risk Category IV and use a higher wind speed map

questions.text: Does the building store or handle hazardous materials that could pose a public risk if released?
questions.why: Hazardous material storage elevates the building to Risk Category III minimum

derivation.confirm_to_user: Based on your answers, this building is classified as Risk Category {risk_category} per ASCE 7-16 Table 1.5-1. This means we will use wind speed map Figure {wind_speed_figure}.

phases.name: Site and Wind Speed
phases.goal: Look up basic wind speed V from JSON based on location and risk category

questions.text: What city and state is the project located in? A zip code also works.
questions.why: Used to look up the basic wind speed (V) from ASCE 7-16 Figure 26.5-1
questions.llm_action: Match to nearest entry in wind_speed_lookup.json using city/state or zip. Return V in mph.

derivation.confirm_to_user: Based on your location, the basic wind speed is {V} mph per ASCE 7-16 Figure {wind_speed_figure}. Does that look correct?
derivation.fallback: If location is not found in lookup table, ask: 'I was not able to automatically determine the wind speed for your location. Please look up the basic wind speed on ASCE 7-16 Figure {wind_speed_figure} and enter the value in mph.'

phases.name: Exposure Category
phases.goal: Determine Exposure B, C, or D based on upwind terrain description

questions.text:
How would you describe the terrain surrounding the building for at least half a mile in most directions? Choose the best match:
  A) Dense urban or suburban area, wooded terrain, or closely spaced structures (houses, trees, small buildings) — Exposure B
  B) Open terrain with scattered obstructions generally less than 30 ft tall (fields, farmland, open lots, grasslands) — Exposure C
  C) Flat, unobstructed area near a large body of water such as ocean, bay, or large lake — Exposure D
questions.why: Exposure category controls how wind speed varies with height (Kz values). A more exposed site = higher wind pressures at all heights.
questions.guidance: If the site is near a boundary between two categories, or if you are unsure, select the less sheltered option (e.g. C over B) for a conservative result.

phases.name: Topographic Feature
phases.goal: Determine if Kzt > 1.0 applies or if we default to 1.0

questions.text: Is the building located on or near a hill, ridge, or escarpment (a cliff-like slope)?
questions.why: Buildings on elevated terrain experience higher wind speeds. If yes, we need a few more measurements to calculate the topographic factor Kzt per ASCE 7-16 §26.8.

questions.text: What type of topographic feature is the building near?
options: 2D Ridge (long ridge running perpendicular to wind)
questions.why: The shape of the feature affects how much the wind speed increases at the crest.

questions.text: What is the height of the topographic feature (H) in feet? This is the vertical distance from the base to the crest.
What is the horizontal distance from the crest to the point upwind where the elevation equals H/2 (called Lh)? This characterizes how steep the feature is.
What is the horizontal distance from the crest of the feature to the building site (x) in feet?

derivation.confirm_to_user: The topographic factor Kzt = {Kzt} has been calculated per ASCE 7-16 Figure 26.8-1.

phases.name: Building Geometry
phases.goal: Collect all dimensional inputs needed for Kz, h/L ratio, and Cp lookups

questions.text: What is the mean roof height (h) of the building in feet? This is measured from grade to the midpoint of the roof slope. For a flat roof, this is just the eave height.
What is the length (L) of the building in feet? This is the horizontal dimension parallel to the wind direction being analyzed.
What is the width (B) of the building in feet? This is the horizontal dimension perpendicular to the wind direction.
questions.note_to_llm: After collecting B, compute and store h/L and L/B ratios — these are needed for Cp lookups

questions.text: What type of roof does the building have?
options: Flat (slope less than 2 degrees or 1/2:12 pitch)
Gable (two slopes meeting at a ridge)
Hip (four sloping sides meeting at a ridge)
questions.why: Roof type and slope determine the external pressure coefficients (Cp) on the roof surfaces.

questions.text: What is the roof slope in degrees? (If you know it as rise:run, divide rise by run and take the arctangent — e.g. 4:12 pitch = 18.4 degrees)
Relative to the wind direction being analyzed, is the wind blowing normal (perpendicular) to the ridge or parallel (along) the ridge?
questions.why: Wind direction relative to the ridge line produces different pressure distributions on the roof slopes.

phases.name: Design Intent
phases.goal: Scope the output before running calculations

options: MWFRS — Main Wind Force Resisting System (overall lateral loads on the structural system)
C&C — Components and Cladding (pressures on individual cladding, windows, roofing, connections)

questions.text: Which design standard are you using?
options: LRFD — Load and Resistance Factor Design
questions.why: The design standard determines which load combination factors apply when combining wind loads with dead, live, and other loads.

phases.name: Input Confirmation
phases.goal: Present all collected inputs to user for review before calculating

summary_template.text: Before I run the calculations, here is a summary of everything I have collected. Please review and confirm — or let me know what to correct.

phases.confirmation_prompt: Does everything look correct? If yes, I will proceed with the calculations. If anything needs to be changed, just let me know which item to update.

calculated_outputs_order: Kh (from Kz table at z = h)
qh (velocity pressure at mean roof height)
qz profile (windward wall — varies with height)
Cp values (walls and roof per geometry)
